6: 
7: ## Research Findings
8: 
9: ### File Paths Verified
10: - ✅ `src/server/payload/collections/Exercises/schemas.ts` — QuestionAxisBlockSchema at lines 417-428, uses `.strict()`
11: - ✅ `src/server/payload/collections/Exercises/types.ts` — QuestionAxisBlock interface at lines 224-233

14: - ✅ `src/ui/admin/ExerciseContentEditor/components/axis/AxisConfigPanel.tsx` — Config panel for axis settings
15: - ✅ `src/ui/web/exerciserenderer/blocks/AxisRenderer/index.tsx` — Student-facing renderer, hardcodes width=600 height=400
16: - ✅ `src/ui/web/exerciserenderer/graphics/JSXGraphBoard.tsx` — Board component, takes `width: number` and `height: number`
17: - ✅ `src/ui/web/exerciserenderer/ExerciseRenderer/index.tsx` — Main renderer, lines 331-336 render question_axis blocks
18: - ✅ `src/ui/admin/ExerciseContentEditor/components/axis/AxisCanvas.tsx` — Admin canvas, also hardcodes 600x400
19: - ✅ `tests/int/contracts/axis-spec.int.spec.ts` — Existing axis schema tests
20: - ✅ `tests/int/contracts/exercise-content-blocks.int.spec.ts` — Existing content block tests
21: 
22: ### Patterns Observed
23: - Schemas use `.strict()` (no unknown keys allowed) — new fields must be added to the Zod schema for validation to pass
24: - TypeScript interfaces in `types.ts` mirror Zod schemas in `schemas.ts` manually (not generated)
25: - `defaults.ts` provides factory functions for each block type with sensible defaults
26: - The admin AxisEditor delegates to AxisConfigPanel for config fields (grid, viewport, labels)
27: - Student-facing AxisRenderer passes fixed pixel `width={600}` and `height={400}` to JSXGraphBoard
28: - JSXGraphBoard accepts `width: number` and `height: number` — sets them as inline style
29: - Both admin and student JSXGraphBoard components use inline `style={{ width, height }}` for sizing
30: 
31: ### Integration Points
32: - QuestionAxisBlockSchema is part of ContentBlockSchema discriminated union (line 534)
33: - Adding a field to QuestionAxisBlockSchema doesn't affect the union discriminator (`type` field)
34: - ExerciseRenderer reads axis blocks at lines 331-336 using `b.axis as AxisSpecV1` — needs to also read `displaySize`
35: 
36: ## Reuse Inventory
37: 
38: ### Existing utilities the plan will reuse
39: - `AxisConfigPanel` from `src/ui/admin/ExerciseContentEditor/components/axis/AxisConfigPanel.tsx` — extend with displaySize selector (same UI pattern: select dropdown + label)
40: - `generateId` from `src/server/payload/collections/Exercises/types.ts` — already used in defaults
41: - `cn` from `src/infra/utils/ui` — for conditional Tailwind classes in renderer
42: - Existing test patterns from `tests/int/contracts/axis-spec.int.spec.ts` and `exercise-content-blocks.int.spec.ts`
43: 
44: ### New code justified
45: - `GraphDisplaySize` type constant — no existing enum/type for display sizes exists
46: - Display size → CSS width mapping utility — no existing sizing utility applies to graphs
47: 
48: ---
49: 
50: ## Step 1: Add `displaySize` to Schema, Types, and Defaults (Backend Contract)
51: 
52: **Spec refs**: Requirement 1 (Size Configuration), Requirement 2 (Default State), Requirement 6 (Size Persistence)
53: 
54: **Files to Touch**:
55: - `src/server/payload/collections/Exercises/schemas.ts` (MODIFIED — lines 417-428)
56: - `src/server/payload/collections/Exercises/types.ts` (MODIFIED — lines 224-233)
57: - `src/server/payload/collections/Exercises/defaults.ts` (MODIFIED — lines 326-347)
58: 
59: **Exact Behavior**:
60: 
61: 1. In `schemas.ts`, add a `DisplaySizeSchema` Zod enum: `z.enum(['small', 'medium', 'large', 'full']).default('full')` representing width percentages (small=33%, medium=50%, large=75%, full=100%).
62: 
63: 2. Add `displaySize: DisplaySizeSchema.optional()` to `QuestionAxisBlockSchema` (line 418, inside the `.object({...})`). Making it `.optional()` with `.default('full')` ensures backward compatibility — existing data without this field will parse as `'full'`.
64: 
65: 3. In `types.ts`, add `displaySize?: 'small' | 'medium' | 'large' | 'full'` to the `QuestionAxisBlock` interface.
66: 
67: 4. In `defaults.ts`, add `displaySize: 'full'` to the `question_axis` factory default (line 327).
68: 
69: **Tests that FAIL before, PASS after**:
70: - Test file: `tests/unit/collections/exercise-display-size.test.ts` (NEW)
71: - Test 1: "QuestionAxisBlockSchema accepts block with displaySize='small'" — parse a valid question_axis block with `displaySize: 'small'` → should NOT throw
72: - Test 2: "QuestionAxisBlockSchema accepts block without displaySize (backward compat)" — parse existing block data without displaySize → should NOT throw, parsed value should default to 'full'
73: - Test 3: "QuestionAxisBlockSchema rejects invalid displaySize value" — parse block with `displaySize: 'tiny'` → should throw ZodError
74: - Test 4: "ContentBlockSchema validates question_axis with displaySize" — parse through ContentBlockSchema union → should NOT throw
75: - **Run**: `pnpm vitest run tests/unit/collections/exercise-display-size.test.ts`
76: 
77: **Acceptance Criteria**:
78: - [ ] `QuestionAxisBlockSchema.parse(blockWithDisplaySize)` succeeds for all valid sizes
79: - [ ] Existing blocks without `displaySize` still parse correctly (defaults to 'full')
80: - [ ] Invalid `displaySize` values are rejected by Zod
81: - [ ] `QuestionAxisBlock` TypeScript interface includes `displaySize?` field
82: - [ ] Default factory creates blocks with `displaySize: 'full'`
83: 
84: ---
85: 
86: ## Step 2: Add Display Size Selector to Admin AxisEditor
87: 
88: **Spec refs**: Requirement 1 (Size Configuration), Requirement 5 (End-User Presentation — save alongside content)
89: 
90: **Files to Touch**:
91: - `src/ui/admin/ExerciseContentEditor/editors/AxisEditor.tsx` (MODIFIED — add displaySize dropdown)
92: 
93: **Exact Behavior**:
94: 
95: 1. Add a "Display Size" section at the top of the AxisEditor, BEFORE the "Prompt" section. This is a block-level setting, not an axis-spec-level setting, so it updates `block.displaySize` directly (not `block.axis`).
96: 
97: 2. Render a `<select>` with options:
98:    - Small (33%) — value `'small'`
99:    - Medium (50%) — value `'medium'`
100:    - Large (75%) — value `'large'`
101:    - Full Width (100%) — value `'full'` (default)
102: 
103: 3. On change, call `onChange({ ...block, displaySize: selectedValue })`.
104: 
105: 4. Use the existing `panel-field` and `panel-field-select` CSS classes from the AxisConfigPanel pattern for consistent styling.
106: 
107: **Tests that FAIL before, PASS after**:
108: - Test file: `tests/unit/ui/axis-editor-display-size.test.ts` (NEW)
109: - Test 1: "AxisEditor renders display size selector with current value" — render AxisEditor with a block that has `displaySize: 'medium'`, verify a select element exists with value 'medium'
110: - Test 2: "AxisEditor calls onChange with updated displaySize when selector changes" — simulate selecting 'small', verify onChange was called with `{ ...block, displaySize: 'small' }`
111: - Test 3: "AxisEditor defaults display size to 'full' when not set" — render AxisEditor with block without displaySize, verify select shows 'full'
112: - **Run**: `pnpm vitest run tests/unit/ui/axis-editor-display-size.test.ts`
113: 
114: **Acceptance Criteria**:
115: - [ ] Admin AxisEditor shows a "Display Size" dropdown
116: - [ ] Changing the dropdown calls onChange with the new displaySize value
117: - [ ] Default selection is "Full Width" when no displaySize is set
118: - [ ] The displaySize value persists after save and reload (tested manually — it's stored in the JSON content)
119: 
120: ---
121: 
122: ## Step 3: Update Student-Facing AxisRenderer to Use `displaySize`
123: 
124: **Spec refs**: Requirement 3 (Proportional Scaling), Requirement 5 (End-User Presentation)
125: 
126: **Files to Touch**:
127: - `src/ui/web/exerciserenderer/blocks/AxisRenderer/index.tsx` (MODIFIED)
128: - `src/ui/web/exerciserenderer/ExerciseRenderer/index.tsx` (MODIFIED — lines 331-336, pass displaySize)
129: 
130: **Exact Behavior**:
131: 
132: 1. **AxisRenderer** — Add a `displaySize` prop (optional, defaults to `'full'`). Create a constant mapping:
133:    ```
134:    const SIZE_MAP = { small: '33%', medium: '50%', large: '75%', full: '100%' } as const
135:    ```
136:    
137: 2. Instead of hardcoding `width={600}` and `height={400}`, use a responsive approach:
138:    - Use a ResizeObserver (or `useRef` + `useEffect`) to measure the container's actual width
139:    - Apply the percentage from SIZE_MAP to determine the actual pixel width
140:    - Calculate height proportionally: `height = measuredWidth * (2/3)` (maintaining 3:2 aspect ratio from original 600x400)
141:    - Pass computed pixel values to JSXGraphBoard
142: 
143: 3. **Simpler approach** (preferred for reliability with JSXGraph which needs pixel values):
144:    - Wrap JSXGraphBoard in a container `<div>` with `style={{ width: SIZE_MAP[displaySize] }}` and `className="mx-auto"`
145:    - Keep JSXGraphBoard at `width={600}` `height={400}` but add `style={{ maxWidth: '100%', height: 'auto', aspectRatio: '3/2' }}` via the existing `className` prop
146:    - Actually: JSXGraph requires explicit pixel width/height for proper rendering. Use a `useRef` + `ResizeObserver` on a wrapper div to measure actual available width, then pass `width={Math.min(600, measuredWidth)}` and `height={Math.min(600, measuredWidth) * 2/3}` to JSXGraphBoard.
147: 
148: 4. **ExerciseRenderer** — At lines 331-336, extract `displaySize` from the block and pass it to AxisRenderer:
149:    ```tsx
150:    const axisBlock = b as ContentBlock & { axis?: AxisSpecV1; displaySize?: string }
151:    <AxisRenderer blockId={b.id} spec={axisBlock.axis as AxisSpecV1} displaySize={axisBlock.displaySize as 'small' | 'medium' | 'large' | 'full' | undefined} />
152:    ```
153: 
154: **Tests that FAIL before, PASS after**:
155: - Test file: `tests/unit/ui/axis-renderer-display-size.test.ts` (NEW)
156: - Test 1: "AxisRenderer container applies correct width percentage for 'small'" — render with displaySize='small', verify wrapper div has `width: 33%` style
157: - Test 2: "AxisRenderer defaults to full width when displaySize is undefined" — render without displaySize, verify wrapper div has `width: 100%`
158: - Test 3: "AxisRenderer maintains aspect ratio at different sizes" — verify the computed height maintains 3:2 ratio relative to width
159: - **Run**: `pnpm vitest run tests/unit/ui/axis-renderer-display-size.test.ts`
160: 
161: **Acceptance Criteria**:
162: - [ ] AxisRenderer renders at the correct width percentage for each size option
163: - [ ] Graph maintains 3:2 aspect ratio regardless of selected size
164: - [ ] Default behavior (no displaySize) renders at full width (backward compatible)
165: - [ ] JSXGraphBoard receives proper pixel dimensions
166: 
167: ---
168: 
169: ## Step 4: Support Side-by-Side Layout (Text + Graph)
170: 
171: **Spec refs**: Requirement 4 (Layout Interaction — Text & Graph)
172: 
173: **Files to Touch**:
174: - `src/ui/web/exerciserenderer/ExerciseRenderer/index.tsx` (MODIFIED — lines 318-337)
175: 
176: **Exact Behavior**:
177: 
178: The exercise content uses a flat block stream. To support side-by-side layout: when a `question_axis` block with `displaySize` != 'full' is followed by (or preceded by) a `rich_text` block, they should be grouped into a flex row where:
179: - The graph takes the configured percentage width
180: - The text fills the remaining space
181: 
182: Implementation approach:
183: 1. In the block rendering loop (line 321), detect when a `question_axis` block with a non-'full' displaySize is adjacent to a `rich_text` block.
184: 2. Group them into a `<div className="flex gap-4 items-start">` container where:
185:    - The axis block wrapper gets `style={{ width: SIZE_MAP[displaySize] }}` with `flex-shrink: 0`
186:    - The rich_text block wrapper gets `className="flex-1 min-w-0"`
187: 3. The grouping logic: look ahead one block — if the current block is `question_axis` with displaySize != 'full' AND the next block is `rich_text`, render them together and skip the next block in the iteration.
188: 
189: **Tests that FAIL before, PASS after**:
190: - Test file: `tests/unit/ui/exercise-renderer-side-by-side.test.ts` (NEW)
191: - Test 1: "renders axis + rich_text side-by-side when displaySize is not full" — provide content with [question_axis(displaySize='medium'), rich_text], verify they share a flex container
192: - Test 2: "renders axis at full width without side-by-side when displaySize is 'full'" — provide content with [question_axis(displaySize='full'), rich_text], verify they render as separate blocks (no flex row)
193: - Test 3: "text fills remaining space in side-by-side layout" — verify the text wrapper has `flex-1` class
194: - **Run**: `pnpm vitest run tests/unit/ui/exercise-renderer-side-by-side.test.ts`
195: 
196: **Acceptance Criteria**:
197: - [ ] Non-full-width axis blocks paired with adjacent rich_text render side-by-side
198: - [ ] Text dynamically fills remaining width (flex-1)
199: - [ ] Full-width axis blocks render normally (no side-by-side)
200: - [ ] Axis blocks without adjacent text blocks still render correctly
201: 
202: ---
203: 
204: ## Step 5: Integration Test — Full Round-Trip
205: 
206: **Spec refs**: All acceptance criteria (AC-1 through AC-6)
207: 
208: **Files to Touch**:
209: - `tests/int/contracts/exercise-content-blocks.int.spec.ts` (MODIFIED — add test cases)
210: 
211: **Exact Behavior**:
212: 
213: Add new test cases to the existing integration test file:
214: 
215: 1. "question_axis block with displaySize validates through ContentSchema" — Create a full exercise content JSON with a question_axis block that has `displaySize: 'medium'`, validate it passes ContentSchema.parse()
216: 2. "question_axis block without displaySize still validates (backward compat)" — Existing question_axis data (no displaySize field) still parses correctly
217: 3. "question_axis block with all displaySize values validates" — Loop through ['small', 'medium', 'large', 'full'] and verify all parse correctly
218: 
219: **Tests**:
220: - Test location: `tests/int/contracts/exercise-content-blocks.int.spec.ts`
221: - **Run**: `pnpm vitest run tests/int/contracts/exercise-content-blocks.int.spec.ts`
222: 
223: **Acceptance Criteria**:
224: - [ ] Full exercise content with displaySize validates through ContentSchema
225: - [ ] Backward compatibility — existing data without displaySize still works
226: - [ ] All predefined size values are accepted by the schema
227: 
228: ---
229: 
230: ## Summary
231: 
232: | Step | Files Changed | Est. Time | Key Test |
233: |------|--------------|-----------|----------|
234: | 1    | schemas.ts, types.ts, defaults.ts | 15 min | Schema validates displaySize values |
235: | 2    | AxisEditor.tsx | 15 min | Admin editor shows size selector |
236: | 3    | AxisRenderer/index.tsx, ExerciseRenderer/index.tsx | 20 min | Student view respects displaySize |
237: | 4    | ExerciseRenderer/index.tsx | 20 min | Side-by-side layout works |
238: | 5    | exercise-content-blocks.int.spec.ts | 10 min | Full round-trip validation |
239: 
240: **Total estimated time**: ~80 minutes
241: 
242: **Quality gates**: `pnpm vitest run` (all new + existing tests), `pnpm tsc --noEmit` (type safety)

input.content:
# Test Agent Report: 260313-auto-475

## Tests Written

- Schema validation tests for `displaySize` field in QuestionAxisBlock
- Admin UI tests for display size selector in AxisEditor
- Student-facing tests for AxisRenderer display size
- Side-by-side layout tests for ExerciseRenderer

## Test Files

| File | Test Count | Type |
|------|-----------|------|
| tests/unit/collections/exercise-display-size.test.ts | 12 | unit |
| tests/unit/ui/axis-editor-display-size.test.tsx | 7 | unit |
| tests/unit/ui/axis-renderer-display-size.test.tsx | 8 | unit |
| tests/unit/ui/exercise-renderer-side-by-side.test.tsx | 6 | unit |

## Test Cases

| Test Name | Type | Expected Behavior |
|-----------|------|-------------------|
| QuestionAxisBlockSchema accepts block with displaySize='small' | unit | Validates schema with small size |
| QuestionAxisBlockSchema accepts block with displaySize='medium' | unit | Validates schema with medium size |
| QuestionAxisBlockSchema accepts block with displaySize='large' | unit | Validates schema with large size |
| QuestionAxisBlockSchema accepts block with displaySize='full' | unit | Validates schema with full size |
| QuestionAxisBlockSchema accepts block without displaySize (backward compat) | unit | Defaults to 'full' |
| QuestionAxisBlockSchema rejects invalid displaySize value | unit | Throws ZodError |
| ContentBlockSchema validates question_axis with displaySize | unit | Passes through union schema |
| ExerciseBlockDefaults question_axis factory creates with displaySize='full' | unit | Default factory has 'full' |
| AxisEditor renders display size selector with current value | unit | Shows current displaySize |
| AxisEditor renders display size selector with all options | unit | Shows all 4 options |
| AxisEditor defaults display size to 'full' when not set | unit | Default selection is 'full' |
| AxisEditor calls onChange with updated displaySize | unit | Handler receives new value |
| AxisRenderer applies 33% width for 'small' | unit | Container has 33% width |
| AxisRenderer applies 50% width for 'medium' | unit | Container has 50% width |
| AxisRenderer applies 75% width for 'large' | unit | Container has 75% width |
| AxisRenderer applies 100% width for 'full' | unit | Container has 100% width |
| AxisRenderer defaults to full width when displaySize undefined | unit | Backward compatible |
| AxisRenderer maintains 3:2 aspect ratio | unit | Board preserves aspect ratio |
| ExerciseRenderer renders axis + rich_text side-by-side | unit | Flex container created |
| ExerciseRenderer does NOT side-by-side when displaySize='full' | unit | Full width layout |
| ExerciseRenderer text wrapper has flex-1 class | unit | Text fills remaining space |

## Notes

- Tests use mock components to isolate behavior testing
- UI tests require `@testing-library/jest-dom` matchers at runtime
- Schema tests validate both valid and invalid inputs
- Backward compatibility tests ensure existing data still works without displaySize field
